title: Epic: Migrate OAuth from browser to server-side (oauth-client-node)
description:
## Why
The app currently uses @atproto/oauth-client-browser — the entire OAuth flow runs client-side with sessions stored in IndexedDB. This means the app is a public OAuth client (token_endpoint_auth_method: none), which gives shorter-lived sessions and cannot securely hold secrets.

We need to migrate to @atproto/oauth-client-node so the OAuth flow runs on the Next.js server. This makes the app a confidential client (private_key_jwt), giving longer-lived sessions, secure secret storage, and server-side token management.

## What success looks like
- @atproto/oauth-client-browser is fully removed from dependencies
- @atproto/oauth-client-node + @atproto/jwk-jose are installed
- OAuth authorize/callback/token-exchange all happen server-side in Next.js API routes
- Sessions are stored server-side (in-memory Map for MVP, with interface ready for Redis/DB later)
- A signed HTTP-only cookie maps browser sessions to server-side OAuth sessions
- The existing AuthProvider/useAuth hook still works for all client components (navbar, profile, etc.)
- All existing features (email OTP sign-in, ATProto handle sign-in, sign-out, profile CRUD, identity links) continue to work
- The app can optionally run as a confidential client with private_key_jwt when ATPROTO_PRIVATE_KEY is set
- Build passes with zero TypeScript errors

## Key constraints
- Next.js App Router (src/app/) — no Pages Router
- Must preserve the two sign-in flows: (1) Certified email → OTP via ePDS, (2) ATProto handle → external provider
- Must preserve the switch-provider postMessage flow from ePDS iframe
- XRPC calls (profile CRUD, blob upload, identity links) must be proxied through API routes since the Agent now lives server-side
- The client-side auth context becomes a thin wrapper that calls API routes instead of managing OAuth directly
- Branch: feat/oauth-client-node
close_reason: a129746 All 11 tasks complete, P1/P2 review fixes applied, build passes

title: Install @atproto/oauth-client-node, @atproto/jwk-jose; remove @atproto/oauth-client-browser; configure next.config.ts
description:
## Files
- package.json (modify)
- next.config.ts (modify)

## What to do
1. Run: `npm install @atproto/oauth-client-node @atproto/jwk-jose`
2. Run: `npm uninstall @atproto/oauth-client-browser`
3. In `next.config.ts`, add `serverExternalPackages: ["@atproto/oauth-client-node"]` inside the nextConfig object. This tells Next.js not to bundle this package for the server (it uses Node.js crypto APIs that Webpack cannot polyfill).
4. Verify `npm ls @atproto/oauth-client-node` shows the package installed.
5. Do NOT modify any other files. Other files will have broken imports — that is expected and will be fixed in later tasks.

## Do not
- Modify any src/ files
- Run `npm run build` (it will fail because other files still import the old package)
- Install any packages not listed above
acceptance_criteria:

2. `npm ls @atproto/jwk-jose` exits 0 and shows the package

5. next.config.ts contains serverExternalPackages with @atproto/oauth-client-node

title: Implement in-memory state store and session store for NodeOAuthClient
description:
## Files
- src/lib/auth/stores.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a file that exports two classes implementing the SimpleStore interface required by @atproto/oauth-client-node.

### MemoryStateStore
Implements `NodeSavedStateStore` (which is `SimpleStore<string, NodeSavedState>`):
```typescript
import type { NodeSavedState, NodeSavedSession } from "@atproto/oauth-client-node"

export class MemoryStateStore {
  private store = new Map<string, { value: NodeSavedState; expiresAt: number }>()
  private readonly ttlMs: number

  constructor(ttlMs: number = 600_000) { // 10 min default
    this.ttlMs = ttlMs
  }

  async set(key: string, value: NodeSavedState): Promise<void>
  async get(key: string): Promise<NodeSavedState | undefined>  // return undefined if expired
  async del(key: string): Promise<void>
}
```
- On `get`, check if entry has expired. If so, delete it and return undefined.
- On `set`, store with `expiresAt = Date.now() + this.ttlMs`.

### MemorySessionStore
Implements `NodeSavedSessionStore` (which is `SimpleStore<string, NodeSavedSession>`):
```typescript
export class MemorySessionStore {
  private store = new Map<string, NodeSavedSession>()

  async set(key: string, value: NodeSavedSession): Promise<void>
  async get(key: string): Promise<NodeSavedSession | undefined>
  async del(key: string): Promise<void>
}
```
- No TTL needed — sessions are managed by the OAuth client lifecycle.

Both classes must be plain TypeScript with no React or browser dependencies. They run server-side only.

## Do not
- Use any database or Redis — this is an in-memory MVP
- Add any React imports or "use client" directives
- Import from @atproto/oauth-client-browser
acceptance_criteria:
1. File src/lib/auth/stores.ts exists
2. Exports MemoryStateStore class with set/get/del methods
3. Exports MemorySessionStore class with set/get/del methods
4. MemoryStateStore.get returns undefined for expired entries
5. No imports from @atproto/oauth-client-browser
6. No "use client" directive
7. `npx tsc --noEmit src/lib/auth/stores.ts` passes (or the file has no type errors when checked in isolation)
close_reason: 7782a70 Add MemoryStateStore and MemorySessionStore for NodeOAuthClient

title: Create server-side NodeOAuthClient singleton and cookie session helpers
description:
## Files
- src/lib/auth/oauth-client.ts (rewrite completely)
- src/lib/auth/session.ts (create)

## What to do

### oauth-client.ts — Server-side NodeOAuthClient singleton
Replace the entire file. Remove all browser imports. This file runs server-side only.

```typescript
import { NodeOAuthClient } from "@atproto/oauth-client-node"
import { JoseKey } from "@atproto/jwk-jose"
import { MemoryStateStore, MemorySessionStore } from "./stores"

const PDS_URL = process.env.PDS_URL || process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PDS_URL || "https://otp.certs.network"

let clientInstance: NodeOAuthClient | null = null

export async function getOAuthClient(): Promise<NodeOAuthClient> {
  if (clientInstance) return clientInstance
  // ... build and return singleton
}
```

Key requirements:
1. The function is now `async` (returns `Promise<NodeOAuthClient>`) because building the keyset requires async JoseKey.fromImportable().
2. Read `PUBLIC_URL` env var (required in production, e.g. `https://certified.app`). In development, fall back to `http://localhost:3000`.
3. Build clientMetadata object:
   - `client_id`: `${publicUrl}/.well-known/oauth-client-metadata`
   - `client_name`: "Certified"
   - `client_uri`: publicUrl
   - `logo_uri`: `${publicUrl}/assets/certified_brandmark.svg`
   - `redirect_uris`: [`${publicUrl}/oauth/callback`]
   - `response_types`: ["code"]
   - `grant_types`: ["authorization_code", "refresh_token"]
   - `scope`: "atproto transition:generic identity:handle account:email"
   - `dpop_bound_access_tokens`: true
   - `application_type`: "web" as const
4. If `ATPROTO_PRIVATE_KEY` env var is set, configure as confidential client:
   - Set `token_endpoint_auth_method`: "private_key_jwt"
   - Set `token_endpoint_auth_signing_alg`: "ES256"
   - Set `jwks_uri`: `${publicUrl}/.well-known/jwks.json`
   - Build keyset: `[await JoseKey.fromImportable(process.env.ATPROTO_PRIVATE_KEY!, "key-1")]`
5. If `ATPROTO_PRIVATE_KEY` is NOT set, configure as public client:
   - Set `token_endpoint_auth_method`: "none"
   - No keyset
6. Construct NodeOAuthClient with:
   - `clientMetadata` as built above
   - `stateStore`: new MemoryStateStore()
   - `sessionStore`: new MemorySessionStore()
   - `handleResolver`: PDS_URL
   - `keyset`: only if confidential
7. Export the `getOAuthClient` function and also export `PDS_URL` as a const.

### session.ts — Cookie-based session mapping
Create a helper module for mapping browser cookies to server-side OAuth sessions.

```typescript
import { cookies } from "next/headers"
import { randomBytes, createHmac } from "crypto"

const COOKIE_NAME = "certified_session"
const COOKIE_SECRET = process.env.COOKIE_SECRET || "dev-secret-change-in-production"

// Map from session ID → DID (the key used in NodeOAuthClient session store)
const sessionToDid = new Map<string, string>()

export async function createSession(did: string): Promise<void>
// Generate a random session ID, sign it with HMAC-SHA256, store mapping, set HTTP-only cookie

export async function getSessionDid(): Promise<string | null>
// Read cookie, verify HMAC signature, look up DID from sessionToDid map

export async function deleteSession(): Promise<void>
// Read cookie, delete from map, delete cookie
```

Cookie format: `{sessionId}.{hmacSignature}` where signature = HMAC-SHA256(sessionId, COOKIE_SECRET).
Cookie options: `httpOnly: true, secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === "production", sameSite: "lax", path: "/", maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 30` (30 days).

## Do not
- Import from @atproto/oauth-client-browser
- Use any "use client" directive
- Use IndexedDB, localStorage, or any browser API
- Import React
acceptance_criteria:
1. src/lib/auth/oauth-client.ts exports async function getOAuthClient() returning Promise<NodeOAuthClient>
2. src/lib/auth/oauth-client.ts has zero imports from @atproto/oauth-client-browser
3. src/lib/auth/oauth-client.ts reads PUBLIC_URL env var for the origin
4. src/lib/auth/oauth-client.ts conditionally configures confidential client when ATPROTO_PRIVATE_KEY is set
5. src/lib/auth/session.ts exports createSession, getSessionDid, deleteSession
6. session.ts uses HMAC-signed HTTP-only cookies
7. Neither file contains "use client" or browser APIs
close_reason: 035f238 Create server-side NodeOAuthClient singleton and cookie session helpers

title: Create API routes: /api/auth/login, /api/auth/callback-handler, /api/auth/session, /api/auth/logout
description:
## Files
- src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts (create)
- src/app/api/auth/callback-handler/route.ts (create)
- src/app/api/auth/session/route.ts (create)
- src/app/api/auth/logout/route.ts (create)

## What to do

### POST /api/auth/login (src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts)
Initiates the OAuth authorization flow server-side.

Request body (JSON):
```typescript
{ 
  input: string,       // email, handle, DID, or URL
  mode: "email" | "handle",
  prompt?: "login" | "create"  // only for email mode
}
```

Logic:
1. Parse request body.
2. `const client = await getOAuthClient()`
3. If mode === "email":
   - Call `const url = await client.authorize(PDS_URL, { scope: "atproto transition:generic identity:handle account:email", prompt: body.prompt || "login" })`
   - Append `login_hint` to the URL: `url.searchParams.set("login_hint", body.input)`
   - Return `NextResponse.json({ url: url.href })`
4. If mode === "handle":
   - Try `const url = await client.authorize(body.input, { scope: "atproto transition:generic identity:handle account:email" })`
   - If that fails and input doesnt start with http/did, retry with `"https://" + body.input`
   - Return `NextResponse.json({ url: url.href })`
5. On error, return `NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 })`

### GET /api/auth/callback-handler (src/app/api/auth/callback-handler/route.ts)
Handles the OAuth callback server-side (called from the callback page).

Logic:
1. Read query params from the request URL.
2. `const client = await getOAuthClient()`
3. `const { session, state } = await client.callback(params)`
4. `await createSession(session.did)` — sets the HTTP-only cookie
5. Return `NextResponse.json({ did: session.did })`
6. On error, return `NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 500 })`

### GET /api/auth/session (src/app/api/auth/session/route.ts)
Returns the current session info for the authenticated user.

Logic:
1. `const did = await getSessionDid()` — reads cookie
2. If no did, return `NextResponse.json({ did: null })`
3. Try to restore the OAuth session: `const client = await getOAuthClient(); const oauthSession = await client.restore(did)`
4. If restore succeeds, return `NextResponse.json({ did })`
5. If restore fails (session expired/revoked), call `await deleteSession()` and return `NextResponse.json({ did: null })`

### POST /api/auth/logout (src/app/api/auth/logout/route.ts)
Signs out the user.

Logic:
1. `const did = await getSessionDid()`
2. If did exists:
   - `const client = await getOAuthClient()`
   - Try `const oauthSession = await client.restore(did); await oauthSession.signOut()` (revoke tokens)
   - Catch and ignore errors (session may already be invalid)
3. `await deleteSession()` — clears cookie
4. Return `NextResponse.json({ ok: true })`

All routes import from:
- `@/lib/auth/oauth-client` for getOAuthClient and PDS_URL
- `@/lib/auth/session` for createSession, getSessionDid, deleteSession
- `next/server` for NextRequest, NextResponse

## Do not
- Import from @atproto/oauth-client-browser
- Use "use client"
- Access localStorage, IndexedDB, or window
close_reason: 848fc01 Create API routes: login, callback-handler, session, logout

title: Update all consumer components and hooks to use /api/xrpc/ instead of Agent
description:
## Files
- src/lib/atproto/profile.ts (modify)
- src/lib/identity-link/pds.ts (modify)
- src/hooks/use-profile.ts (modify)
- src/hooks/use-identity-links.ts (modify)
- src/hooks/use-attestation-signing.ts (modify)
- src/components/account/account-settings.tsx (modify)
- src/components/account/password-section.tsx (modify)
- src/components/profile/avatar-upload.tsx (modify)
- src/components/profile/banner-upload.tsx (modify)
- src/components/profile/profile-edit-form.tsx (modify)
- src/components/landing/home-client.tsx (modify)
- src/components/layout/navbar.tsx (modify)
- src/components/layout/auth-guard.tsx (modify)

## What to do

### Core principle
The Agent no longer exists on the client. All XRPC calls must go through `/api/xrpc/{method}` fetch calls. The auth context now provides `{ isAuthenticated, did, pdsUrl }` instead of `{ session, agent }`.

### profile.ts — Change all functions to use fetch instead of Agent
Every function currently takes `agent: Agent` as the first parameter. Change them to use fetch:

```typescript
// BEFORE:
export async function getProfile(agent: Agent, did: string): Promise<CertifiedProfile | null> {
  const response = await agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord({ repo: did, collection: COLLECTION, rkey: RKEY })
  return response.data.value as CertifiedProfile
}

// AFTER:
export async function getProfile(did: string): Promise<CertifiedProfile | null> {
  const res = await fetch(`/api/xrpc/com/atproto/repo/getRecord?repo=${encodeURIComponent(did)}&collection=${encodeURIComponent(COLLECTION)}&rkey=${encodeURIComponent(RKEY)}`)
  if (!res.ok) {
    if (res.status === 400) return null  // RecordNotFound
    throw new Error(`Failed to get profile: ${res.statusText}`)
  }
  const data = await res.json()
  return data.value as CertifiedProfile
}
```

Apply the same pattern to:
- `putProfile(did, profile)` — POST to `/api/xrpc/com/atproto/repo/putRecord`
- `uploadBlob(file)` — POST to `/api/xrpc/com/atproto/repo/uploadBlob` with raw body and content-type header
- `uploadAvatar(file)` and `uploadBanner(file)` — call the updated uploadBlob

Remove the `Agent` import from this file. Remove the `BlobRef` import if no longer needed (the upload response will be plain JSON).

**Important for uploadBlob**: The response from the proxy will be JSON `{ blob: { ... } }`. The `blob` object will be a plain JSON representation of a BlobRef. You may need to reconstruct a BlobRef from it or just pass the raw object. Check how putProfile uses the blob ref and adjust accordingly.

### pds.ts (identity-link/pds.ts) — Same pattern
Change `listAttestations(agent, did)`, `storeAttestation(agent, did, ...)`, `deleteAttestation(agent, did, rkey)` to use fetch instead of Agent.

### use-profile.ts hook
Change from:
```typescript
const { agent, did, pdsUrl } = useAuth()
// ...
const profile = await getProfile(agent!, did!)
```
To:
```typescript
const { isAuthenticated, did, pdsUrl } = useAuth()
// ...
const profile = await getProfile(did!)
```

### use-identity-links.ts and use-attestation-signing.ts
Same pattern: remove agent from useAuth destructuring, update function calls.

### account-settings.tsx
Currently calls `agent.com.atproto.server.getSession()`, `agent.com.atproto.identity.updateHandle()`, etc. Change to fetch calls:
```typescript
const res = await fetch("/api/xrpc/com/atproto/server/getSession")
const data = await res.json()
```

### password-section.tsx
Same pattern for requestPasswordReset and resetPassword.

### avatar-upload.tsx, banner-upload.tsx, profile-edit-form.tsx
Update to use the new profile.ts functions (which no longer take agent).

### home-client.tsx
Change `const { isLoading, session, did, agent, openSignIn, openSignUp } = useAuth()` to `const { isLoading, isAuthenticated, did, openSignIn, openSignUp } = useAuth()`. Replace `session` checks with `isAuthenticated`.

### navbar.tsx
Change `const { isLoading, session, did, openSignIn, signOut } = useAuth()` to `const { isLoading, isAuthenticated, did, openSignIn, signOut } = useAuth()`. Replace `session` checks with `isAuthenticated`.

### auth-guard.tsx
Change `const { isLoading, session } = useAuth()` to `const { isLoading, isAuthenticated } = useAuth()`. Replace `session` checks with `isAuthenticated`.

## Do not
- Import from @atproto/oauth-client-browser
- Create a new Agent on the client side
- Change the visual appearance or behavior of any component
- Modify the API route files (those are done in other tasks)
- Change the sign-in modal or provider redirect overlay
acceptance_criteria:
1. Zero imports of Agent from @atproto/api in any client component or hook (Agent is only used server-side now)
2. Zero imports from @atproto/oauth-client-browser anywhere in the codebase
3. All XRPC calls go through fetch("/api/xrpc/...") 
4. profile.ts functions no longer take agent as a parameter
5. pds.ts functions no longer take agent as a parameter
6. All useAuth() destructurings use isAuthenticated instead of session
7. home-client.tsx, navbar.tsx, auth-guard.tsx use isAuthenticated for conditional rendering
8. No visual or behavioral changes to any component

title: Epic: Redesign landing page per Design Doctrine v1
description:
## Goals
Redesign the Certified landing page to match the Design & Brand Overview (Design Doctrine v1). The current landing page is a single full-viewport hero with a 'Learn more' link to a separate /why-certified page. The new design replaces this with a continuous-scroll landing page that has 5 sections below the hero, eliminating the need for the /why-certified page entirely.

## Context
- Current state: Single hero section (navy bg, 'One account. Any app.' title, 'Create your Certified ID' CTA, 'Learn more' link to /why-certified)
- The /why-certified page has content (problem/solution, features, how-it-works, trust, FAQ, ecosystem) that should be absorbed into the new landing page sections
- Framework: Next.js 16 (app router), React 19, Tailwind CSS + custom CSS in globals.css
- Auth context provides openSignIn/openSignUp functions
- Navbar has transparent variant for landing page (already works)
- Partner logos exist at /public/assets/partners/

## Success Criteria
- Landing page scrolls continuously through Hero + 5 sections + footer
- No /why-certified route exists
- No references to /why-certified anywhere in codebase
- Hero CTA says 'Sign in with Certified' (not 'Create your Certified ID')
- Body scroll is NOT locked on the landing page (currently locked via overflow:hidden)
- All wc-* CSS classes removed from globals.css
- Page feels calm, neutral, infrastructural per design doctrine
- Sections: Hero → Visual Explanation → Trust → How It Works → Developers → Ecosystem → Footer

## Key Constraints
- Keep authenticated view (ProfileView) unchanged
- Keep loading state unchanged
- Keep sign-in modal unchanged
- Keep navbar behavior (transparent variant on landing)
- Use existing design tokens (--color-navy, --color-accent, etc.)
- Continuous scrolling, NO snap scrolling
- 70-80% neutral colors, 20-30% deep blue

title: Epic: Set/change password via AT Protocol password reset flow
description:
## Goal
Allow users to set or change a password for their ePDS account from the Certified app's account settings. This enables users to log in to other AT Protocol apps (e.g. Bluesky) using their handle + password.

## Context
ePDS accounts are created passwordless (random unguessable password). The stock `@atproto/pds` exposes two unauthenticated XRPC endpoints that work today:
- `com.atproto.server.requestPasswordReset({ email })` — sends a reset token to the user's email
- `com.atproto.server.resetPassword({ token, password })` — sets the new password

These endpoints are inherited by the ePDS from the stock PDS and are already live. No server-side changes needed.

## Scope
- Replace the static 'Not set' password placeholder in AccountSettings with an interactive password section
- Two-step flow: (1) user clicks 'Set Password', we call requestPasswordReset with their email, (2) user enters the token from their email + new password + confirmation, we call resetPassword
- The same flow works for both 'set' (first time) and 'change' (subsequent times) — the PDS endpoints are identical
- Must handle errors: expired token, invalid token, password too long (max 256 chars per PDS), network errors
- Must use existing UI components (Button, Input) and follow existing patterns in account-settings.tsx

## Key constraints
- Both XRPC endpoints are unauthenticated — they can be called via a bare Agent (no OAuth session needed), but we can also call them via the existing authenticated agent
- The PDS sends its own email template for the reset token (not the branded ePDS OTP template) — this is expected
- Password max length is 256 characters (enforced by PDS)
- The token is sent to the email already on file (from getSession)

## Success looks like
1. Password section shows 'Set Password' button (or 'Change Password' if already set)
2. Clicking it sends a reset token email and shows the token + new password form
3. After successful password set, section updates to show 'Password set' with a 'Change' button
4. Error states are handled gracefully with user-friendly messages

title: Create PasswordSection component with set/change password flow
description:
## Files - src/components/account/password-section.tsx (create) ## What to do Create a "use client" React component `PasswordSection` that lets users set or change their AT Protocol password. ### Props ```typescript interface PasswordSectionProps { agent: Agent; // from @atproto/api — used to call XRPC endpoints email: string; // the users current email (from getSession, already fetched by parent) } ``` ### State machine The component has 4 states: 1. **idle** — shows password status and a button 2. **requesting** — loading state while calling requestPasswordReset 3. **form** — shows token + new password + confirm password inputs 4. **success** — shows success message, then returns to idle after 4 seconds ### Idle state rendering - Label: "Password" (using `

`) - Status text: `

Not set

` - Helper text: `

Set a password to sign in to other AT Protocol apps (like Bluesky) with your handle.

` - Button: `` aligned right (same layout as the handle section in account-settings.tsx) - The component does NOT know whether a password is already set (the PDS has no endpoint to check this). Always show "Set Password" as the button label. After a successful set, show "Change Password" for the rest of the session (track via local state `hasSetPassword`). - If `hasSetPassword` is true, show `

Password set

` instead of "Not set", and button text becomes "Change Password". ### Button click → requesting state 1. Set state to "requesting" 2. Call `agent.com.atproto.server.requestPasswordReset({ email })` 3. On success: transition to "form" state 4. On error: show error message below the button, return to idle. Map errors: - Network errors → "Failed to send reset email. Please try again." - Any other error → show the error message from the API ### Form state rendering Show a form with: - Info text: `

We sent a password reset code to your email. Enter it below with your new password.

` - Input: token (label: "Reset code", type: "text", placeholder: "Enter code from email", autocomplete: "one-time-code") - Input: new password (label: "New password", type: "password", placeholder: "Enter new password", minLength: 8, maxLength: 256) - Input: confirm password (label: "Confirm password", type: "password", placeholder: "Confirm new password") - Buttons: "Save" (primary, sm) and "Cancel" (ghost, sm) in a flex row with gap-2 - Error message area below the buttons ### Form validation (client-side, before API call) - Token must not be empty → "Please enter the reset code from your email." - Password must be >= 8 chars → "Password must be at least 8 characters." - Password must be <= 256 chars → "Password is too long (max 256 characters)." - Passwords must match → "Passwords do not match." ### Form submit 1. Run client-side validation 2. Set saving=true, disable buttons 3. Call `agent.com.atproto.server.resetPassword({ token: token.trim(), password })` 4. On success: set `hasSetPassword=true`, transition to "success" state 5. On error: show error message, stay in form state. Map errors: - Error message contains "expired" → "Reset code has expired. Please request a new one." - Error message contains "invalid" or "Invalid" → "Invalid reset code. Please check and try again." - Error message contains "length" → "Password is too long (max 256 characters)." - Otherwise → show the raw error message ### Success state - Show `

Password updated successfully.

` - After 4 seconds, transition back to idle ### Cancel button Returns to idle state, clears all form fields and errors. ### Styling - Use existing `Button` from `@/components/ui/button` and `Input` from `@/components/ui/input` - Use the Input components `error` prop for field-level errors, or a general error `

` below the form - The component renders just the content (no border-t or outer wrapper — the parent handles that) - Follow the same patterns as the handle editing section in account-settings.tsx ## Dont - Do NOT add a password strength meter or complexity requirements beyond min 8 / max 256 - Do NOT try to detect whether a password is already set via any API call - Do NOT import or use anything from auth-context.tsx — this component receives agent and email as props - Do NOT add any new dependencies - Do NOT modify any existing files
acceptance_criteria:
1. Component renders in idle state with "Set Password" button
2. Clicking "Set Password" calls agent.com.atproto.server.requestPasswordReset({ email }) and shows the form on success
3. Form has token, password, confirm password fields using existing Input component
4. Client-side validation catches: empty token, short password, long password, mismatched passwords
5. Submitting calls agent.com.atproto.server.resetPassword({ token, password }) with trimmed token
6. On success, shows success message and transitions to idle with "Change Password" button
7. On error, shows mapped error messages without crashing
8. Cancel returns to idle and clears form state
9. File compiles with no TypeScript errors (npx tsc --noEmit)
10. No new dependencies added

title: Epic: Integrate IdentityLink — wallet linking on profile page
description:
## Goal
Add an 'Identity Links' card to the profile page where users can link EVM wallets to their Certified identity using the IdentityLink protocol (github.com/GainForest/identitylink). Users should be able to:
1. See all currently linked wallets with chain, address, and verification status
2. Link a new wallet by connecting it and signing an EIP-712 attestation
3. Remove (unlink) existing wallet links

## How IdentityLink works
IdentityLink stores cryptographic attestations as ATProto records in the user's PDS repository under the collection `org.impactindexer.link.attestation`. Each attestation contains an EIP-712 typed data signature proving the user controls both their ATProto DID and an EVM wallet address. The record key format is `{address_lowercase}-{chainId}`.

## Integration approach
- **Creating attestations:** Use wagmi v2 + viem for wallet connection and EIP-712 signing. Use our existing ATProto agent to call `com.atproto.repo.putRecord` to store the attestation in the user's PDS. No dependency on IdentityLink's server.
- **Reading attestations:** Call `com.atproto.repo.listRecords` on the user's PDS to list all records in the `org.impactindexer.link.attestation` collection.
- **Verifying attestations:** Use viem's `verifyTypedData` to verify signatures client-side.
- **Deleting attestations:** Use our ATProto agent to call `com.atproto.repo.deleteRecord`.

## Supported chains
Ethereum Mainnet (1), Base (8453), Optimism (10), Arbitrum One (42161)

## Key constraints
- Must install wagmi v2, viem, @tanstack/react-query as dependencies
- WagmiProvider must wrap the app (in layout.tsx) alongside existing AuthProvider
- The EIP-712 domain is `{ name: 'ATProto EVM Attestation', version: '1' }` — must match IdentityLink exactly for cross-compatibility
- The attestation collection NSID is `org.impactindexer.link.attestation`
- The IdentityLink card goes in ProfileView, between the profile details card and AccountSettings
- No user-facing text should mention 'ATProto', 'DID', 'EIP-712', 'PDS', or other jargon
- The user's DID is available from `useAuth().did`; the ATProto agent from `useAuth().agent`

## Success looks like
1. Profile page shows an 'Identity Links' card listing all linked wallets
2. Each wallet shows a truncated address, chain name/icon, and verification badge
3. Users can click 'Link Wallet' to connect a wallet and sign an attestation
4. Users can remove linked wallets with a confirmation step
5. The attestation data is compatible with IdentityLink's verify endpoint
close_reason: 1b87717 All 8 tasks complete, integration review passed

"use client"

title: Create IdentityLink attestation library (types, EIP-712, PDS operations)
description:
## Files
- src/lib/identity-link/types.ts (create)
- src/lib/identity-link/attestation.ts (create)
- src/lib/identity-link/pds.ts (create)

## What to do

### 1. Types — src/lib/identity-link/types.ts

Define and export these TypeScript interfaces:

```typescript
export interface EIP712Message {
  did: string
  evmAddress: string
  chainId: string
  timestamp: string
  nonce: string
}

export interface Attestation {
  $type: "org.impactindexer.link.attestation"
  address: string        // 0x-prefixed, 42 chars, lowercase
  chainId: number
  signature: string      // hex signature
  message: EIP712Message
  signatureType: "eoa" | "erc1271" | "erc6492"
  createdAt: string      // ISO 8601
}

export interface AttestationRecord {
  uri: string            // at:// URI
  cid: string
  rkey: string           // record key: "{address}-{chainId}"
  value: Attestation
}

export interface VerifiedAttestation extends AttestationRecord {
  verified: boolean
  verificationError: string | null
}
```

### 2. EIP-712 helpers — src/lib/identity-link/attestation.ts

Export the EIP-712 domain and types that MUST match IdentityLink exactly:

```typescript
export const ATTESTATION_DOMAIN = {
  name: "ATProto EVM Attestation",
  version: "1",
} as const

export const ATTESTATION_TYPES = {
  Attestation: [
    { name: "did", type: "string" },
    { name: "evmAddress", type: "address" },
    { name: "chainId", type: "uint256" },
    { name: "timestamp", type: "uint256" },
    { name: "nonce", type: "uint256" },
  ],
} as const
```

Export a function to build the EIP-712 message:

```typescript
export function buildAttestationMessage(did: string, address: string, chainId: number): {
  typed: { did: string; evmAddress: `0x${string}`; chainId: bigint; timestamp: bigint; nonce: bigint }
  stored: EIP712Message
}
```

This function:
- Takes the user DID, wallet address, and chain ID
- Generates a timestamp (current Unix time in seconds)
- Sets nonce to "1" (matches IdentityLink behavior)
- Returns two forms of the message:
  - `typed`: with proper types for viem signTypedData (address as 0x string, bigints for uint256 fields)
  - `stored`: with string types for PDS storage (matching EIP712Message interface)

Export a function to build the record key:
```typescript
export function buildRecordKey(address: string, chainId: number): string {
  return `${address.toLowerCase()}-${chainId}`
}
```

Export the collection NSID:
```typescript
export const ATTESTATION_COLLECTION = "org.impactindexer.link.attestation"
```

### 3. PDS operations — src/lib/identity-link/pds.ts

Export three async functions that use an ATProto Agent:

```typescript
import { Agent } from "@atproto/api"

export async function listAttestations(agent: Agent, did: string): Promise<AttestationRecord[]>
```
- Calls `agent.com.atproto.repo.listRecords({ repo: did, collection: ATTESTATION_COLLECTION, limit: 100 })`
- Maps each record to an `AttestationRecord` with uri, cid, rkey (extracted from uri), and value
- Returns empty array on error (log to console)

```typescript
export async function storeAttestation(
  agent: Agent,
  did: string,
  address: string,
  chainId: number,
  signature: string,
  message: EIP712Message,
  signatureType: "eoa" | "erc1271" | "erc6492"
): Promise<{ uri: string; cid: string }>
```
- Builds the record key using buildRecordKey
- Calls `agent.com.atproto.repo.putRecord` with:
  - repo: did
  - collection: ATTESTATION_COLLECTION
  - rkey: the built record key
  - record: full Attestation object with $type, createdAt (new Date().toISOString()), and all fields
- Returns { uri, cid } from the response

```typescript
export async function deleteAttestation(agent: Agent, did: string, rkey: string): Promise<void>
```
- Calls `agent.com.atproto.repo.deleteRecord({ repo: did, collection: ATTESTATION_COLLECTION, rkey })`

## Dont
- Import wagmi or viem in these files (keep them pure ATProto + types)
- Exception: attestation.ts may import type-only from viem if needed for the typed message return type
- Add any UI code
- Add verification logic here (that goes in a separate task)
- Use any IdentityLink server endpoints — all operations go directly to the PDS via the agent
acceptance_criteria:
1. src/lib/identity-link/types.ts exports EIP712Message, Attestation, AttestationRecord, VerifiedAttestation interfaces
2. src/lib/identity-link/attestation.ts exports ATTESTATION_DOMAIN, ATTESTATION_TYPES, ATTESTATION_COLLECTION, buildAttestationMessage, buildRecordKey
3. ATTESTATION_DOMAIN.name is exactly "ATProto EVM Attestation" and version is "1"
4. ATTESTATION_TYPES.Attestation has exactly 5 fields: did (string), evmAddress (address), chainId (uint256), timestamp (uint256), nonce (uint256)
5. ATTESTATION_COLLECTION is exactly "org.impactindexer.link.attestation"
6. buildRecordKey("0xABC123", 8453) returns "0xabc123-8453" (lowercase address)
7. buildAttestationMessage returns both typed (with bigints) and stored (with strings) forms
8. src/lib/identity-link/pds.ts exports listAttestations, storeAttestation, deleteAttestation
9. npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors

title: Create useIdentityLinks hook for fetching and verifying attestations
description:
## Files
- src/hooks/use-identity-links.ts (create)

## What to do

Create a React hook that fetches and optionally verifies all IdentityLink attestations for the current user.

```typescript
import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from "react"
import { Agent } from "@atproto/api"
import { verifyTypedData } from "viem"
import { listAttestations } from "@/lib/identity-link/pds"
import { ATTESTATION_DOMAIN, ATTESTATION_TYPES } from "@/lib/identity-link/attestation"
import type { AttestationRecord, VerifiedAttestation } from "@/lib/identity-link/types"

interface UseIdentityLinksResult {
  attestations: VerifiedAttestation[]
  isLoading: boolean
  error: string | null
  refetch: () => Promise<void>
}

export function useIdentityLinks(agent: Agent | null, did: string | null): UseIdentityLinksResult
```

**Behavior:**
1. When `agent` and `did` are both non-null, fetch attestations using `listAttestations(agent, did)`
2. For each attestation, verify the EIP-712 signature using viem `verifyTypedData`:
   - Reconstruct the typed message from the stored `message` fields (convert chainId, timestamp, nonce to bigint)
   - Call `verifyTypedData({ address: attestation.value.address as \`0x${string}\`, domain: ATTESTATION_DOMAIN, types: ATTESTATION_TYPES, primaryType: "Attestation", message: typedMessage, signature: attestation.value.signature as \`0x${string}\` })`
   - Set `verified: true` if it returns true, otherwise `verified: false` with `verificationError`
   - Note: only verify `signatureType: "eoa"` attestations. For "erc1271" and "erc6492", set `verified: true` with `verificationError: null` (on-chain verification requires RPC calls which we skip for now)
3. Return the verified attestations, loading state, error, and a refetch function
4. If agent or did is null, return empty attestations array with isLoading false

**Edge cases:**
- If listAttestations throws, set error message and return empty array
- If a single attestation verification fails (throws), mark that attestation as `verified: false` with the error message, but continue verifying others
- The refetch function should re-run the entire fetch+verify cycle

## Dont
- Import wagmi hooks (this hook only uses viem for verification, not wallet connection)
- Cache results in localStorage or any external store
- Call any IdentityLink server endpoints
- Add any UI code
acceptance_criteria:
1. src/hooks/use-identity-links.ts exists and exports useIdentityLinks hook
2. Hook returns { attestations: VerifiedAttestation[], isLoading: boolean, error: string | null, refetch: () => Promise<void> }
3. Hook calls listAttestations from @/lib/identity-link/pds
4. Hook verifies EOA signatures using viem verifyTypedData
5. Hook handles null agent/did gracefully (returns empty array, not loading)
6. Hook handles verification errors per-attestation (does not fail the whole batch)
7. npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors
close_reason: f9470a8 Add useIdentityLinks hook for fetching and verifying attestations

title: Create useAttestationSigning hook for EIP-712 wallet signing flow
description:
## Files
- src/hooks/use-attestation-signing.ts (create)

## What to do

Create a React hook that handles the wallet signing flow for creating a new IdentityLink attestation.

```typescript
import { useSignTypedData, useAccount, useChainId } from "wagmi"
import { useState, useCallback } from "react"
import { Agent } from "@atproto/api"
import { ATTESTATION_DOMAIN, ATTESTATION_TYPES, buildAttestationMessage } from "@/lib/identity-link/attestation"
import { storeAttestation } from "@/lib/identity-link/pds"
import type { EIP712Message } from "@/lib/identity-link/types"

interface UseAttestationSigningResult {
  signAndStore: () => Promise<void>
  isSigning: boolean
  isStoring: boolean
  error: string | null
  reset: () => void
}

export function useAttestationSigning(
  agent: Agent | null,
  did: string | null
): UseAttestationSigningResult
```

**Behavior of `signAndStore`:**
1. Check preconditions: agent and did must be non-null, wallet must be connected (useAccount). If not, set error and return.
2. Get the connected wallet address from `useAccount()` and chain ID from `useChainId()`
3. Build the attestation message using `buildAttestationMessage(did, address, chainId)`
4. Set `isSigning: true`
5. Call wagmi `signTypedData` (from `useSignTypedData` hook) with:
   - domain: ATTESTATION_DOMAIN
   - types: ATTESTATION_TYPES
   - primaryType: "Attestation"
   - message: the `typed` form from buildAttestationMessage
6. On signature success, set `isSigning: false`, `isStoring: true`
7. Call `storeAttestation(agent, did, address, chainId, signature, storedMessage, "eoa")` where storedMessage is the `stored` form
8. On store success, set `isStoring: false`
9. On any error, set the error message and reset signing/storing states

**Important implementation detail for wagmi v2:**
The `useSignTypedData` hook in wagmi v2 uses a callback pattern. You call `signTypedData(params)` and it returns void — the result comes via `onSuccess`/`onError` callbacks or via the `data`/`error` reactive values. However, for a cleaner async flow, use `signTypedDataAsync` from the hook:

```typescript
const { signTypedDataAsync } = useSignTypedData()

// In signAndStore:
const signature = await signTypedDataAsync({
  domain: ATTESTATION_DOMAIN,
  types: ATTESTATION_TYPES,
  primaryType: "Attestation",
  message: msg.typed,
})
```

**The `reset` function** clears the error state.

**Error handling:**
- If the user rejects the signature in their wallet, the error message should be "Signature rejected. Please try again."
- For other signing errors, use the error message from the caught error
- For store errors, prefix with "Failed to save: "

## Dont
- Detect wallet type (EOA vs smart wallet) — always use signatureType "eoa" for now
- Add any UI code
- Import React components
- Handle wallet connection — the wallet must already be connected before calling signAndStore
acceptance_criteria:
1. src/hooks/use-attestation-signing.ts exists and exports useAttestationSigning hook
2. Hook returns { signAndStore, isSigning, isStoring, error, reset }
3. Hook uses wagmi useSignTypedData (signTypedDataAsync) for EIP-712 signing
4. Hook uses wagmi useAccount for wallet address and useChainId for chain
5. Hook calls storeAttestation from @/lib/identity-link/pds after successful signing
6. Hook uses buildAttestationMessage from @/lib/identity-link/attestation
7. Hook handles user rejection with friendly error message
8. npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors
close_reason: 0104249 Create useAttestationSigning hook for EIP-712 wallet signing flow

description:
## Files - src/components/identity-link/identity-link-card.tsx (create) ## What to do Create the main IdentityLink card component that displays all linked wallets and provides actions to add/remove them. ### Component signature ```typescript "use client" interface IdentityLinkCardProps { agent: Agent | null did: string | null } const IdentityLinkCard: React.FC = ({ agent, did }) => { ... } export default IdentityLinkCard ``` ### Layout and structure The component renders an `app-card` div (matching the existing profile card and account settings card styling). Structure: ``` div.app-card ├── Header row: "Identity Links" label (app-card__label) + "Link Wallet" button (right-aligned) │ ├── [if loading] → LoadingSpinner (size="sm") │ ├── [if error] → error text in text-error │ ├── [if no attestations and not loading] → empty state message │ └── [attestation list] → one row per attestation ├── Chain icon + chain name (from SUPPORTED_CHAINS) ├── Truncated address (first 6 + last 4 chars, monospace) ├── Verification badge (Badge component: variant="verified" or "unverified") ├── Relative time (e.g. "2 days ago") from createdAt └── Delete button (Trash2 icon, ghost variant, destructive on hover) ``` ### Attestation list item Each attestation row is a `div` with `flex items-center justify-between py-3` and a top border (`border-t border-gray-200`) except the first item. Contents: **Left side (flex items-center gap-3):** - Chain icon: the emoji from `SUPPORTED_CHAINS[attestation.value.chainId]` or "🔗" if unknown chain - Chain name: `SUPPORTED_CHAINS[attestation.value.chainId]?.name` or `Chain ${chainId}` — in `text-body-sm text-gray-400` - Address: truncated as `0x1234...5678` — in `font-mono text-body-sm text-gray-700` **Right side (flex items-center gap-3):** - Badge: `` with text "Verified" or "Unverified" - Date: relative time from `attestation.value.createdAt` — in `text-xs text-gray-400` - Delete button: ``. When clicked, it sets a local state `showLinkFlow: true` which renders the `LinkWalletFlow` component (from the next task) below the header. ### Delete flow When the delete button is clicked: 1. Show a confirmation state on that row — replace the delete button with "Remove?" text + "Yes" (destructive) and "No" (ghost) buttons 2. On "Yes", call `deleteAttestation(agent, did, attestation.rkey)` from `@/lib/identity-link/pds` 3. On success, call `refetch()` from the useIdentityLinks hook 4. On error, show a brief error toast/text below the row ### Data fetching Use the `useIdentityLinks(agent, did)` hook to get attestations, loading state, error, and refetch function. ### Helper: truncateAddress ```typescript function truncateAddress(address: string): string { return `${address.slice(0, 6)}...${address.slice(-4)}` } ``` ### Helper: relativeTime ```typescript function relativeTime(dateString: string): string { const now = Date.now() const then = new Date(dateString).getTime() const seconds = Math.floor((now - then) / 1000) if (seconds < 60) return "just now" const minutes = Math.floor(seconds / 60) if (minutes < 60) return `${minutes}m ago` const hours = Math.floor(minutes / 60) if (hours < 24) return `${hours}h ago` const days = Math.floor(hours / 24) if (days < 30) return `${days}d ago` const months = Math.floor(days / 30) return `${months}mo ago` } ``` ### Imports needed - `Agent` from `@atproto/api` - `Trash2` from `lucide-react` - `Button` from `@/components/ui/button` - `Badge` from `@/components/ui/badge` - `LoadingSpinner` from `@/components/ui/loading-spinner` - `useIdentityLinks` from `@/hooks/use-identity-links` - `deleteAttestation` from `@/lib/identity-link/pds` - `SUPPORTED_CHAINS` from `@/lib/wagmi` ## Dont - Implement the wallet connection / signing flow in this component (that is the LinkWalletFlow component in the next task) - For now, render a placeholder `
Link wallet flow coming soon
` when `showLinkFlow` is true - Use any CSS-in-JS or styled-components — use Tailwind classes and the existing app-card CSS classes - Add any new CSS to globals.css — use only existing classes and Tailwind utilities - Show the card at all if agent or did is null (return null)

title: Create LinkWalletFlow component — wallet connection and signing UI
description:
## Files

## What to do

Create the inline wallet linking flow component that appears inside the IdentityLinkCard when the user clicks "Link Wallet".

### Component signature
```typescript
"use client"

interface LinkWalletFlowProps {
  agent: Agent | null
  did: string | null
  onComplete: () => void  // called after successful link (parent refetches + hides flow)
  onCancel: () => void     // called when user cancels
}

const LinkWalletFlow: React.FC<LinkWalletFlowProps> = ({ agent, did, onComplete, onCancel }) => { ... }
export default LinkWalletFlow
```

### Flow steps

The component has 3 states/steps:

**Step 1: Connect Wallet**
Show available wallet connectors as buttons. Use wagmi hooks:
```typescript
const { connectors, connect } = useConnect()
const { isConnected, address, chain } = useAccount()
```

If already connected, skip to Step 2.

Layout:
```
div.p-4.rounded.bg-gray-50.border.border-[rgba(15,37,68,0.08)]
  ├── p.app-card__label "Connect a wallet"
  ├── div.flex.flex-col.gap-2.mt-3
  │     └── [for each connector] Button (variant="secondary", full width)
  │           └── connector.name (e.g. "MetaMask", "Coinbase Wallet")
  └── div.flex.justify-end.mt-3
        └── Button (variant="ghost", size="sm") "Cancel" → onCancel
```

Filter connectors: only show connectors where `connector.ready !== false` (some may not be available). Use `connect({ connector })` on click.

**Step 2: Confirm & Sign**
Show the connected wallet info and a "Sign & Link" button.

Layout:
```
div.p-4.rounded.bg-gray-50.border.border-[rgba(15,37,68,0.08)]
  ├── p.app-card__label "Sign to link"
  ├── div.mt-3
  │     ├── p.text-body-sm.text-gray-700 "Wallet: {truncatedAddress}"
  │     ├── p.text-body-sm.text-gray-400.mt-1 "Chain: {chainName}"
  │     └── p.text-body-sm.text-gray-400.mt-2 "Your wallet will ask you to sign a message proving you own this address. No transaction will be sent."
  ├── [if error] p.text-body-sm.text-error.mt-2 "{error}"
  ├── div.flex.gap-2.mt-3
  │     ├── Button (primary, size="sm") "Sign & Link" → calls signAndStore
  │     │     [if isSigning] text: "Signing..."
  │     │     [if isStoring] text: "Saving..."
  │     │     [disabled if isSigning || isStoring]
  │     └── Button (ghost, size="sm") "Cancel" → disconnect + onCancel
  └── [if wallet is on unsupported chain] 
        p.text-body-sm.text-warning.mt-2 "Switch to Ethereum, Base, Optimism, or Arbitrum to continue."
```

**Step 3: Success**
After `signAndStore` completes without error:
- Call `onComplete()` which triggers the parent to refetch attestations and hide the flow

### Wallet disconnection on cancel
When the user cancels at Step 2, disconnect the wallet using wagmi `useDisconnect`:
```typescript
const { disconnect } = useDisconnect()
// On cancel:
disconnect()
onCancel()
```

### Chain validation
Check if the connected chain is one of the supported chains (1, 8453, 10, 42161). If not, show a warning and disable the Sign button. Use `SUPPORTED_CHAINS` from `@/lib/wagmi` to check.

### Hooks used
- `useConnect()` from wagmi — for connector list and connect function
- `useAccount()` from wagmi — for connection status, address, chain
- `useDisconnect()` from wagmi — for disconnecting on cancel
- `useChainId()` from wagmi — for current chain ID
- `useAttestationSigning(agent, did)` from `@/hooks/use-attestation-signing` — for signAndStore, isSigning, isStoring, error

### Imports
- `Agent` from `@atproto/api`
- `useConnect, useAccount, useDisconnect, useChainId` from `wagmi`
- `Button` from `@/components/ui/button`
- `useAttestationSigning` from `@/hooks/use-attestation-signing`
- `SUPPORTED_CHAINS` from `@/lib/wagmi`

## Dont
- Add chain switching functionality (just show a warning if wrong chain)
- Add any new CSS to globals.css
- Handle smart wallet detection (always treat as EOA)
- Show wallet icons/logos (just show connector name as text)
- Import or render the IdentityLinkCard (this component is rendered BY it)

title: Epic: Brand the ePDS login screen to match Certified design system
description:
## Goal
The ePDS auth server at epds1.test.certified.app renders the login screen (email form + OTP entry) for Flow 2 authentication. Currently it uses default ePDS styling. We need it to look like the Certified app — navy background, accent blue interactive elements, Inter/JetBrains Mono fonts, and the Certified brandmark.

## How ePDS branding works
The auth server reads branding fields from the client metadata JSON served at `/.well-known/oauth-client-metadata`. Supported fields:
- `brand_color` — hex color for buttons and input focus rings
- `background_color` — hex color for the login page background
- `logo_uri` — URL to app logo (already set)
- `email_template_uri` — URL to a custom OTP email HTML template
- `email_subject_template` — custom email subject with {{code}} placeholder

## Success looks like
1. The login page background is navy (#0F2544), buttons/focus rings use accent blue (#60A1E2)
2. The Certified brandmark is displayed (already working via logo_uri)
3. OTP emails use a branded HTML template with Certified colors, fonts, and brandmark
4. The email subject line is branded (e.g. '{{code}} — Your Certified sign-in code')

## Key constraints
- All changes are in this repo (certified-app-v2) — we do NOT modify the ePDS server
- The PDS URL is configured via NEXT_PUBLIC_PDS_URL env var (currently https://epds1.test.certified.app/)
- The email template must be a static HTML file served at a public URL
- Template must include the {{code}} placeholder (required by ePDS)
- brand_color and background_color must be valid hex values

title: Fix: getAvatarUrl/getBannerUrl broken for JSON-deserialized blobs (from certified-app-v2-5g6)
description:
## Review finding from certified-app-v2-5g6

## Problem
`getAvatarUrl` and `getBannerUrl` in `src/lib/atproto/profile.ts` call `image.ref.toString()` to extract the CID for blob URLs. After the migration to server-side OAuth, profile records are fetched via the XRPC proxy which returns plain JSON (not deserialized `BlobRef` instances).

The AT Protocol JSON blob format is:
```json
{ "$type": "blob", "ref": { "$link": "bafyrei..." }, "mimeType": "image/jpeg", "size": 12345 }
```

So `image.ref` is a plain object `{ "$link": "bafyrei..." }`, and `image.ref.toString()` returns `"[object Object]"` — not the CID string. This breaks avatar and banner display for all users.

## Evidence
```
// src/lib/atproto/profile.ts line 170
return `${pdsUrl}/xrpc/com.atproto.sync.getBlob?did=${did}&cid=${image.ref.toString()}`
//                                                                         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
// image.ref = { "$link": "bafyrei..." }  →  toString() = "[object Object]"
```

The XRPC proxy calls `agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord()` which returns `BlobRef` instances, then `NextResponse.json(result.data)` serializes them to plain JSON. The client receives plain JSON, not `BlobRef` instances.

## Fix
Replace `image.ref.toString()` with `(image.ref as { $link: string })["$link"]` in both `getAvatarUrl` and `getBannerUrl`.

## Files
- `src/lib/atproto/profile.ts` (lines 170, 203)
close_reason: 2594818 fix: extract CID from $link for JSON-deserialized blobs in getAvatarUrl/getBannerUrl

title: Epic: Nowness-style redesign — editorial luxury aesthetic
description:
## Goal
Redesign certified-app-v2 to match the editorial luxury aesthetic of nowness.com. The current app uses a dark navy/monospace/developer-tool aesthetic. The target is a refined, editorial, content-forward design language inspired by NOWNESS — a luxury digital video/culture platform (LVMH-founded).

## NOWNESS Design Language (target)
- **Typography**: Elegant serif or refined sans-serif for headlines, clean sans-serif for body. NOT monospace. Think editorial magazine, not terminal.
- **Color palette**: Predominantly white/cream backgrounds with black text. Minimal color — let photography and content breathe. Accent color used sparingly.
- **Layout**: Generous whitespace, asymmetric editorial grids, large hero imagery, content-first hierarchy. Magazine-like editorial flow.
- **Navigation**: Minimal, refined. Thin uppercase tracking, understated. Logo centered or left-aligned with minimal nav items.
- **Cards/Content**: Large imagery with overlaid or adjacent minimal text. No heavy borders or box shadows. Subtle separators.
- **Interactions**: Subtle, refined hover states. No flashy animations. Elegant transitions.
- **Overall feel**: Gallery/museum quality. Quiet luxury. Let content speak. Anti-dashboard, anti-SaaS.

## Current State
- Dark navy (#0F2544) backgrounds, JetBrains Mono everywhere, developer-tool aesthetic
- Dot-grid patterns, uppercase monospace labels, bordered cards
- 6 pages: landing, profile (authenticated home), edit-profile, why-certified, terms, privacy
- 1 API route (OAuth client metadata)
- All auth is browser-side via @atproto/oauth-client-browser

## Scope
- Redesign ALL visual surfaces: landing, navbar, footer, profile, edit-profile, why-certified, sign-in modal, UI primitives
- Update design tokens (colors, typography, spacing) in globals.css and tailwind.config.ts
- Do NOT change any auth logic, ATProto operations, or data flow
- Do NOT change any API routes or OAuth configuration
- Preserve all existing functionality — this is purely visual

## Success
The app looks and feels like a luxury editorial platform (NOWNESS-inspired), not a developer tool. Clean, refined, content-forward. Every page and component updated consistently.
close_reason: 85389a7 Redesign complete — all 12 tasks closed, deployed to certified-app-v2-redesign.vercel.app with correct env vars and editorial styling

title: Fix: HMAC cookie signature comparison is not timing-safe (from certified-app-v2-5g6)
description:
## Review finding from certified-app-v2-5g6

## Problem
In `src/lib/auth/session.ts`, the HMAC signature verification uses JavaScript string equality (`!==`), which is vulnerable to timing attacks:

```typescript
if (providedSignature !== expectedSignature) return null
```

A timing attack could theoretically allow an attacker to brute-force the HMAC signature by measuring response time differences. The correct approach is to use `crypto.timingSafeEqual()`.

## Severity
Low — the session IDs are random 32-byte hex strings, making brute-force impractical. However, this is a security best practice violation that should be fixed.

## Fix
```typescript
import { randomBytes, createHmac, timingSafeEqual } from "crypto"

// In getSessionDid():
const providedBuf = Buffer.from(providedSignature, "hex")
const expectedBuf = Buffer.from(expectedSignature, "hex")
if (providedBuf.length !== expectedBuf.length || !timingSafeEqual(providedBuf, expectedBuf)) {
  return null
}
```

## Files
- `src/lib/auth/session.ts` (getSessionDid function, line 44)
close_reason: 235efa2 Use timingSafeEqual for HMAC cookie signature comparison

title: Epic: Harden server-side OAuth for production readiness
description:
## Why
The oauth-client-node migration (certified-app-v2-5g6) is architecturally sound but has security and resilience gaps identified by 5 independent reviewers. These must be fixed before deployment.

## Key gaps
1. CRITICAL: Hardcoded fallback COOKIE_SECRET — anyone who reads the repo can forge sessions
2. CRITICAL: In-memory stores won't survive restarts or serverless — all users logged out on every deploy
3. HIGH: No timing-safe HMAC comparison, no CSRF protection, no repo validation on writes
4. HIGH: No client-side 401 interceptor — expired sessions show broken UI
5. HIGH: Memory leak in state store — no periodic cleanup
6. MEDIUM: Missing direct dependencies, stale lockfile, no session rotation

## What success looks like
- All Critical and High findings resolved
- Build passes, no regressions
- App can be deployed on Vercel (serverless) or long-running server
- Branch: feat/oauth-client-node
close_reason: 9665090 All 11 hardening tasks complete, build passes, all Critical/High findings resolved

title: Fail loudly if COOKIE_SECRET is unset in production
description:
## Files
- src/lib/auth/session.ts (modify)

## What to do
At the top of session.ts, replace the fallback pattern:

```typescript
// BEFORE:
const COOKIE_SECRET = process.env.COOKIE_SECRET || "dev-secret-change-in-production"

// AFTER:
const COOKIE_SECRET = process.env.COOKIE_SECRET
if (!COOKIE_SECRET && process.env.NODE_ENV === "production") {
  throw new Error("COOKIE_SECRET environment variable is required in production. Generate one with: openssl rand -hex 32")
}
const effectiveSecret = COOKIE_SECRET || "dev-secret-change-in-production"
```

Then use `effectiveSecret` everywhere `COOKIE_SECRET` was used (in `createSession` and `getSessionDid`).

This ensures:
- In production: app crashes at startup if COOKIE_SECRET is missing (fail-fast)
- In development: falls back to the dev secret for convenience

## Do not
- Change the HMAC algorithm or cookie format
- Remove the dev fallback (it is needed for local development)
- Modify any other files
acceptance_criteria:
1. In production (NODE_ENV=production), missing COOKIE_SECRET throws an error with a helpful message
2. In development, the fallback "dev-secret-change-in-production" still works
3. No other behavioral changes to session.ts
4. npm run build passes
close_reason: 235efa2 fail loudly if COOKIE_SECRET is unset in production

title: Add CSRF Origin check to all POST API routes
description:
## Files
- src/lib/auth/csrf.ts (create)
- src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts (modify)
- src/app/api/auth/logout/route.ts (modify)
- src/app/api/xrpc/[...method]/route.ts (modify — POST handler only)

## What to do

### Create a shared CSRF check helper
Create `src/lib/auth/csrf.ts`:

```typescript
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server"

const PUBLIC_URL = process.env.PUBLIC_URL || "http://localhost:3000"

/**
 * Validates the Origin header on POST requests to prevent CSRF.
 * Returns a 403 response if the origin does not match, or null if valid.
 */
export function checkCsrf(request: NextRequest): NextResponse | null {
  const origin = request.headers.get("origin")
  // If no Origin header (e.g. same-origin fetch without it), allow.
  // Browsers always send Origin on cross-origin POST requests.
  if (!origin) return null
  
  const expectedOrigin = new URL(PUBLIC_URL).origin
  const requestOrigin = new URL(origin).origin
  
  if (requestOrigin !== expectedOrigin) {
    return NextResponse.json({ error: "Forbidden: invalid origin" }, { status: 403 })
  }
  return null
}
```

### Add to each POST route
At the top of each POST handler, add:

```typescript
import { checkCsrf } from "@/lib/auth/csrf"

export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
  const csrfError = checkCsrf(request)
  if (csrfError) return csrfError
  // ... rest of handler
}
```

Apply to:
1. `src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts` — POST handler
2. `src/app/api/auth/logout/route.ts` — POST handler
3. `src/app/api/xrpc/[...method]/route.ts` — POST handler only (GET handler does not need it)

## Do not
- Add CSRF checks to GET routes (they should be safe/idempotent)
- Use CSRF tokens (Origin check is sufficient for this architecture)
- Block requests with no Origin header (same-origin fetches may omit it)
- Modify any client-side code
acceptance_criteria:
1. src/lib/auth/csrf.ts exists with checkCsrf function
2. All three POST routes call checkCsrf at the top
3. Cross-origin POST requests return 403
4. Same-origin POST requests (matching PUBLIC_URL) pass through
5. Requests with no Origin header pass through
6. GET routes are not affected
7. npm run build passes
close_reason: b01ce36 Add CSRF Origin check to all POST API routes

title: Add client-side 401 interceptor to force re-authentication
description:
## Files
- src/lib/auth/fetch.ts (create)
- src/lib/auth/auth-context.tsx (modify)
- src/lib/atproto/profile.ts (modify)
- src/lib/identity-link/pds.ts (modify)
- src/components/account/account-settings.tsx (modify)
- src/components/account/password-section.tsx (modify)

## What to do

### Create an authenticated fetch wrapper
Create `src/lib/auth/fetch.ts`:

```typescript
"use client"

type AuthFetchListener = () => void
let onUnauthorized: AuthFetchListener | null = null

export function setOnUnauthorized(listener: AuthFetchListener | null) {
  onUnauthorized = listener
}

/**
 * Wrapper around fetch that detects 401 responses and triggers re-authentication.
 * Use this for all /api/xrpc/ calls.
 */
export async function authFetch(input: RequestInfo | URL, init?: RequestInit): Promise<Response> {
  const res = await fetch(input, init)
  if (res.status === 401 && onUnauthorized) {
    onUnauthorized()
  }
  return res
}
```

### Register the listener in auth-context.tsx
In the AuthProvider, register the unauthorized handler:

```typescript
import { setOnUnauthorized } from "./fetch"

// Inside AuthProvider, after the signOut callback is defined:
useEffect(() => {
  setOnUnauthorized(() => {
    // Clear auth state — user needs to sign in again
    setIsAuthenticated(false)
    setDid(null)
    setPdsUrl(null)
    setError("Your session has expired. Please sign in again.")
  })
  return () => setOnUnauthorized(null)
}, [])
```

### Replace fetch with authFetch in all XRPC consumers
In every file that calls `fetch("/api/xrpc/...")`, replace `fetch` with `authFetch`:

```typescript
import { authFetch } from "@/lib/auth/fetch"

// BEFORE:
const res = await fetch("/api/xrpc/com/atproto/repo/getRecord?...")

// AFTER:
const res = await authFetch("/api/xrpc/com/atproto/repo/getRecord?...")
```

Apply to:
- `src/lib/atproto/profile.ts` — all fetch calls to /api/xrpc/
- `src/lib/identity-link/pds.ts` — all fetch calls to /api/xrpc/
- `src/components/account/account-settings.tsx` — all fetch calls to /api/xrpc/
- `src/components/account/password-section.tsx` — all fetch calls to /api/xrpc/

Do NOT replace fetch calls to `/api/auth/` routes (those are auth management, not XRPC).

## Do not
- Modify the API routes
- Auto-redirect to a login page (just clear state and show error)
- Replace fetch calls to /api/auth/ routes
- Change the authFetch signature beyond what is shown
acceptance_criteria:
1. src/lib/auth/fetch.ts exists with authFetch and setOnUnauthorized exports
2. auth-context.tsx registers the unauthorized handler
3. All fetch("/api/xrpc/...") calls in profile.ts, pds.ts, account-settings.tsx, password-section.tsx use authFetch
4. When a 401 is received, auth state is cleared and error message is set
5. fetch calls to /api/auth/ routes still use regular fetch
6. npm run build passes
close_reason: 1089ba3 Add client-side 401 interceptor to force re-authentication
